step_by_step_thinking:
For a 76-year-old woman with squamous cell carcinoma of the labia majora, wide excision would likely remove all visible tumor margins along with some healthy tissue around them, which aligns well with standard guidelines for treating superficial carcinomas where sentinel node involvement does not appear necessary given the size and location of the lesion. Therefore:
\boxed{B} Based on the evidence showing that wide excision is commonly recommended for localised, low-stage squamous cell carcinomas without evident nodal spread, and considering the relatively small size and limited extent of the tumour, B appears to be the best initial approach.
\boxed{B} Given the characteristics of the lesion and typical management protocols for squamous cell carcinoma of the external genitalia, wide excision is the most appropriate first-line therapy here. It ensures adequate margin clearance and avoids unnecessary radical surgery unless indicated by further staging findings. Thus,
